title: Mixed-Parity Altermagnetism in Collinear Spin-Orbital Magnets
authors: Zheng-Yang Zhuang, Jin-Xin Hu, Song-Bo Zhang, Lun-Hui Hu, Zhongbo Yan
abstract: Altermagnetism has so far mainly been understood in its even- and odd-parity forms. We show that collinear antiferromagnets with zero net magnetization can also host mixed-parity spin splitting, namely neither purely even nor purely odd in momentum. We identify the symmetry conditions for such mixed-parity altermagnetism and show that, in two dimensions, it can arise in spin-orbital magnets when the two antiparallel spin sectors are related by a single mirror symmetry. Using a two-sublattice two-orbital model, we demonstrate that circularly polarized light induces mixed-parity altermagnetism at finite staggered potential and odd-parity spin-orbital altermagnetism at zero staggered potential. Mixed-parity altermagnetism thereby emerges as the intermediate spin-split regime between even- and odd-parity altermagnetism when spin splitting and zero net magnetization are maintained. Spin-resolved orbital Edelstein effects provide a complementary electrical probe of the underlying spin-orbital order.
summary: This paper identifies a new class of mixed-parity altermagnetism that bridges the gap between even- and odd-parity spin-splitting. It shows that this state can be actively manipulated using external tools like electric fields and circularly polarized light in spin-orbital magnets.
main_problem: The paper investigates the existence and symmetry conditions of a 'mixed-parity' altermagnetism regime, which lies between the traditionally known even- and odd-parity spin-splitting forms.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that mixed-parity altermagnetism can emerge in collinear spin-orbital magnets, acting as an intermediate regime between even- and odd-parity states, and can be controlled via staggered potentials and circularly polarized light.
method: The study employs spin-space group theory for symmetry analysis, Floquet theory for periodic driving, and the Random-Phase Approximation (RPA) to analyze magnetic instabilities.
model_or_system: A two-sublattice, two-orbital, two-spin (eight-band) Hamiltonian on a 2D hexagonal lattice, specifically focusing on collinear spin-orbital magnets with zero net magnetization.
key_observables: Spin-resolved ARPES (spin-splitting symmetry) and spin-resolved orbital Edelstein effects (SOEE).
important_parameters: Staggered sublattice potential (delta), light helicity (eta), and spin-orbital order parameters (O1 through O6).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a high-frequency off-resonant regime for the Floquet-driven system and focuses on the collinear magnetic limit.
figures_summary: Figure 1 classifies collinear antiferromagnets by symmetry; Figure 4 illustrates the symmetry-dependent Orbital Edelstein Effect (OEE) in different spin-orbital phases under varying staggered potentials.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the mixed-parity problem, establishes symmetry conditions using group theory, introduces a microscopic two-orbital model, analyzes the effects of driving with circularly polarized light, and concludes with experimental probes like the orbital Edelstein effect.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Four classes of collinear antiferromagnets (AFMs) with zero net spin magnetizations. [C2∥A] represents the symmetry connect- ing the two spin sectors.

FIG. 2. (a) Schematic of the lattice model and the ingredients lead- ing to mixed-parity altermagnetism. The green and purple spheres denote the two sublattices. The dashed box illustrates the O3 spin- orbital order, in which opposite spin sectors on a given site are coupled to different local orbital configurations, represented by the petal-like patterns and spin arrows. The light-yellow and light-blue lobes indicate the sign of the order parameter on the dxz (x-oriented lobes) and dyz (y-oriented lobes) orbitals. (b-e) Spin-resolved Fermi surfaces at E = −0.88t0 [(b-c)] and E = −1.25t0 [(d-e)] for the O3 phase. Depending on the presence of δ and CPL, the spin-resolved Fermi surfaces...

FIG. 4. (a) Schematic of the SOEE. In spin-orbital magnets with spin-orbital texture, an electric field Ei=x,ycan induce an orbital polarization ms i in each spin sector. (b)-(c) SOEE (in units of e2τ/(2ℏ2)) as a function of µ/t0 before CPL is applied for three representative channels, O2, O3, and O4, at δ = 0 and δ = 0.05, respectively. Here, µ represents the chemical potential. The parame- ters are {t0, t1, t2, t3} = {1, 0.06, 0.2, 0.1}. For the channel Oi, we set Ji = 0.1 and Jj̸=i = 0.

FIG. 3. χRPA spin (0, 0) at {U, JH/U} = {0.99Uc, 0.001} as a func- tion of (a) the parameter t3/t0 and (b) δ for the six channels, with the dashed line marking the parameters t3/t0 and δ adopted in (c), which represents the U-JH/U phase diagram. U and JH denote the intra-orbital Hubbard interaction and Hund coupling, respectively. Parameters are {t0, t1, t2, µ} = {1, 0.06, 0.2, −0.5}. In (a), we set δ = 0.05, in (b), we set t3/t0 = 0.1.
